title: Job History Logging Interface
description: Hadoop's Job History Logging Interface utilizes Avro-generated event classes, defined in `HistoryEvent.java`, to manage job history data. This interface interacts with YARN's timeline service records and standard Java classes, employing Java's generics and Hadoop annotations. It serves as a contract for handling event data, converting to TimelineEvents, and retrieving associated metrics. The interface doesn't have explicit inputs/outputs but manages Avro-generated event data, producing TimelineEvent objects. This system ensures scalability and maintainability through a well-defined interface pattern, enabling extensibility and consistency. Technologies used include Avro for data serialization, Java for programming, and Hadoop annotations for framework integration. The architectural role is primarily that of a coordinator, managing job history data across the cluster.
raw_response:
TITLE: Job History Logging Interface

DESCRIPTION: Hadoop's Job History Logging Interface utilizes Avro-generated event classes, defined in `HistoryEvent.java`, to manage job history data. This interface interacts with YARN's timeline service records and standard Java classes, employing Java's generics and Hadoop annotations. It serves as a contract for handling event data, converting to TimelineEvents, and retrieving associated metrics. The interface doesn't have explicit inputs/outputs but manages Avro-generated event data, producing TimelineEvent objects. This system ensures scalability and maintainability through a well-defined interface pattern, enabling extensibility and consistency. Technologies used include Avro for data serialization, Java for programming, and Hadoop annotations for framework integration. The architectural role is primarily that of a coordinator, managing job history data across the cluster.
